title: Non-string role value silently coerces to 'member'
description: Trigger: members patch value {"role": 42} (integer role). value.get("role").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) returns None; unwrap_or("member") supplies "member" which passes VALID_ROLES check. The write succeeds with role="member" silently. Pre-existing in apply_member_patch before this commit; the new validate_member_patch duplicates the same silent coercion, so atomicity is preserved between phases (consistent behavior). Fix: use ok_or_else to return invalidArguments when role is present but not a string. Not introduced by this commit but the function docs say it validates the value.
